name: Partial IGF1R deficiency extended mouse lifespan
summary: IGF1R heterozygosity provides classic animal lifespan support for reduced insulin/IGF signaling.
statement: Igf1r heterozygous mice showed increased average lifespan and greater resistance to oxidative stress, with the clearest lifespan effect in females.
quantitative_note: The source reports 26% average lifespan extension overall, 33% in females, and a nonsignificant 16% increase in males.
caveats: This is germline genetic mouse evidence, not adult pharmacologic modulation.
The sex-specific effect cautions against treating IGF1R attenuation as uniformly beneficial.
Mouse lifespan extension does not establish human aging benefit.
